questions.stem: Find the transfer function G(s) = I2(s)/V(s) for the circuit given in Figure Q1 (R1, L, R2, C in a ladder network with voltage source v(t)), using nodal analysis.
common_errors: Incorrect node equation setup — missing current through L or C
Forgetting to express I2 in terms of node voltages before taking Laplace
questions.solution_method: Assign node voltages at the junctions between R1-L and L-R2. Write KCL at each node in the s-domain. Solve the resulting algebraic equations for I2(s)/V(s).
key_formulas: KCL: sum of currents leaving node = 0

questions.stem: Write the differential equation governing the dynamic behaviour of the rotational mechanical system shown in Fig. Q2 (J=1 kg·m², two springs K=1 N·m/rad each, two dampers B=1 N·m·s/rad each, torque input T(t), outputs θ1 and θ2). Also find the transfer function G(s) = θ2(s)/T(s).
common_errors: Missing the damper between θ2 and ground when writing the second equation
Sign error on the coupling spring/damper torque terms
questions.solution_method: Apply Newton's 2nd law for rotation to each inertia. Write coupled ODEs: Jθ̈1 = T - B(θ̇1-θ̇2) - K(θ1-θ2) and Jθ̈2 = B(θ̇1-θ̇2) + K(θ1-θ2) - Bθ̇2 - Kθ2. Take Laplace, eliminate Θ1(s) to get Θ2(s)/T(s).

questions.stem: Write the differential equation governing the dynamic behaviour of the translational mechanical system shown in Fig. Q3 (M1=M2=1 kg, K=1 N/m, fv1=fv2=fv3=fv4=1 N·s/m, force input F(t) on M1, output X2). Also find the transfer function G(s) = X2(s)/F(s).
common_errors: Omitting one of the viscous dampers acting on M1 (fv1 and fv2 both connect M1 to ground)
Not accounting for all dampers on M2 (fv3 coupling + fv4 to ground)
questions.solution_method: Apply Newton's 2nd law to each mass. M1: M1ẍ1 = F - fv1ẋ1 - fv2ẋ1 - K(x1-x2) - fv3(ẋ1-ẋ2). M2: M2ẍ2 = K(x1-x2) + fv3(ẋ1-ẋ2) - fv4ẋ2. Take Laplace and solve for X2(s)/F(s).

questions.stem: A rectangular bar (mass m, MI about CG = I) is supported at its two ends by springs k1 and k2. An external force F(t) is applied at distance c from CG (total half-span: a to left, b to right of CG). Bar initially horizontal, weight negligible. Find the transfer function θ(s)/F(s), where θ is CW rotation about the CG.
common_errors: Incorrect moment arm for spring forces (using full length instead of a and b)
Sign error: restoring moment from springs must oppose rotation
Forgetting that the force F(t) also contributes a moment F·c about the CG
Not linearizing the spring deflection for small θ (vertical displacement ≈ aθ, bθ)
questions.solution_method: For small θ, spring deflections are: left spring δ1 = aθ, right spring δ2 = bθ. Net CW moment about CG: M_net = F·c - k1·a·(aθ) - k2·b·(bθ) = F·c - (k1a² + k2b²)θ. Apply rotational Newton's law: Iθ̈ = F·c - (k1a² + k2b²)θ. Laplace: (Is² + k1a² + k2b²)Θ(s) = c·F(s), so Θ(s)/F(s) = c / (Is² + k1a² + k2b²).

notes: Single-page tutorial, 4 problems. All problems are mathematical modelling (electrical and mechanical systems). No marks assigned. Q4 is a non-standard combined translational+rotational problem requiring small-angle linearization — flag as a higher-difficulty modelling type.
